original_abstract:
In this study, green chemical synthesis was employed via pressurized microwave irradiation to investigate the reactivity of 1-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-3-(4-methylphenyl)prop-2-en-1-one (a 2′-hydroxychalcone) toward various active methylene reagents. Reactions with ethyl cyanoacetate, acetylacetone, and thioglycolic acid at 70 °C successfully yielded novel cyanopyridone, acetylcyclohexanone, and thieno[2,3-c]chromen-4-one derivatives, respectively. Additionally, oxidative cyclization of the starting chalcone using hydrogen peroxide produced a substituted flavonol. The chemical structures of all synthesized heterocycles were rigorously characterized using FT-IR, 1H NMR, 13C NMR, and mass spectrometry. Biological evaluation via DPPH assay revealed that several of the synthesized compounds, particularly the flavonol derivative, exhibit significant antioxidant activity comparable to ascorbic acid (Vitamin C), likely due to the presence of radical-stabilizing hydroxyl groups.

To further understand the molecular basis of this activity, molecular docking simulations were performed against human peroxiredoxin 5 (1HD2) and Klebsiella pneumoniae HpxO (3RP8). The results demonstrated that the flavonol derivative possesses high binding affinity and favorable bond lengths within the protein pockets, aligning with the experimental observations. Computational analysis using Density Functional Theory (DFT/B3LYP/6-31G(d,p)) provided insights into the physical descriptors, HOMO–LUMO energy gaps, and chemical stability of the molecules. Furthermore, the flavonol structure was independently confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and Hirshfeld surface analysis, showing an excellent correlation between theoretical predictions and experimental geometric data. This multidisciplinary approach highlights the efficiency of microwave-assisted synthesis in developing potent new antioxidant scaffolds.
humanized_abstract:
Herein, we investigated the reactivity of 1-(2-hydroxyphenyl)-3-(4-methylphenyl)prop-2-en-1-one (2′-hydroxychalcone) toward various active methylene reagents using green chemical synthesis method named as pressurized microwave-assisted technique and obtained diverse heterocyclic derivatives. Using ethyl cyanoacetate, acetylacetone and thioglycolic acid at 70 °C, novel cyanopyridone, acetylcyclohexanone and thieno[2,3-c]chromen-4-one derivatives were obtained in good yields. Oxidative cyclization of the starting chalcone using hydrogen peroxide afforded a substituted flavonol. The FT-IR, 1H NMR, 13C NMR and MS spectra of all the synthesized heterocyclic derivatives were elucidated. Through the DPPH assay, it was seen that some of the synthesized compounds exhibited remarkable antioxidant activity in which the obtained flavonol derivative showed almost the same antioxidant activity as that of standard ascorbic acid (Vitamin C) due to the presence of hydroxyl radical-stabilizing groups.

In order to get insight into the molecular interactions responsible for the observed activity, the flavonol derivative was docked into the active site of human peroxiredoxin 5 (1HD2) and Klebsiella pneumoniae HpxO (3RP8) enzymes using computational models based on experimental X-ray crystal structures. It was found that the derivative showed excellent binding affinities with favorable interactions of the right lengths, as confirmed by experiments. The physical, chemical properties that help identify the molecular properties of compounds at the quantum level were evaluated using Density Functional Theory (DFT/B3LYP/6-31G(d,p)) in order to evaluate HOMO-LUMO energy gaps and stability of compounds. Single-crystal X-ray diffraction studies along with comprehensive analysis using Hirshfeld surface provided clear insight in to the structural features of flavonol and comparisons are made between the theoretical models and experimentally obtained geometric parameters that further confirms the efficacy of the MW reaction conditions in synthesis of potent antioxidant mimics.

document.output:
